select_when: The next move depends on knowing what kind of uncertainty is present because some parts are measurable risk and other parts are reducible ignorance.
One branch of the problem needs probabilistic sizing or simulation while another branch needs discovery work, diligence, or model revision.
Confidence, commitment size, and reversibility need to stay linked instead of being treated as separate decisions.
The team is about to compress all uncertainty into one category even though different tools are required for different unknowns.
avoid_when: Uncertainty classification is becoming theater and is not changing the actual tool choice, commitment size, or monitoring rule.
The problem is routine and predictable enough that the full aleatory-versus-epistemic distinction would mostly add cognitive cost without changing action.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model on the core distinction between irreducible variability and reducible ignorance, with routing tied to tool choice and commitment sizing.
donor_drops: Did not widen into general uncertainty philosophy because the raw source is strongest on classification-linked tool selection, outside-view calibration, and reversibility.
open_questions: The typed output compresses several decision surfaces, including simulation, diligence, and no-regrets sequencing.

select_when.note: The strengths and playbook sections explicitly support mixed uncertainty types, different action tools, and linking confidence to commitment.

avoid_when.note: Both avoid conditions come directly from the danger language on classification theater and the limitation section on low-uncertainty overkill.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on mixed uncertainty problems, while the compact type label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The playbook yields different actions by uncertainty class; the output label compresses that into an operational split.

reasoning_types.note: The model combines probabilistic treatment of risk with diagnosis of ignorance and tool mismatch, while the taxonomy labels remain normalized.
